versions.note: Sees each beat in isolation — no timing context

changed: First end-to-end build: a 77k-parameter 1D CNN trained on ~100,000 MIT-BIH heartbeats, classifying each beat into the 4 AAMI classes.
Split the data by patient (not by beat) so the honest inter-patient result is measured, not an inflated one.
Exported to a self-contained ONNX model (325 KB) running on-device in the browser demo.
improved: Established a strong baseline on ventricular beats: 86% recall on unseen patients.
Macro-recall of 44% clears the 'always say normal' floor of 25%.
knownGaps: Only 9% recall on supraventricular beats: the model reads beat shape but not beat timing, and timing is what defines that class. This is the target of v2.

inProgress: v2 — adding RR-interval (beat-timing) features, which is the signal a model needs to recognise supraventricular beats. Target: lift S-class recall well above v1's 8.8%.

epochSeries.label: Validation macro-recall during training — and why it flatters the model
